channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles fire zones are now facing mudslide threats
item.description: Officials are warning that mudslides could hit the areas affected by the fires as a rainy end to the weekend is expected.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: ICE begins 'enhanced targeted operations' in Austin
item.description: A series of ICE raids were conducted in Austin on Sunday, according to sources from the Drug Enforcement Administration.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, KRON4

item.title: Acting deputy AG head visits Chicago to 'observe' immigration enforcement
item.description: Chicago residents, especially in immigrant circles, have already been on edge for months in anticipation of large-scale immigration arrests promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, KTLA 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Connecticut to award nearly $6 million to family of disabled man wrongfully imprisoned for murder conviction
item.description: Connecticut is set to pay nearly $5.9 million to the family of a disabled man who was wrongly imprisoned in his 1992 murder conviction before he was freed in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man arrested for allegedly calling for Trump assassination on Facebook; Secret Service investigating
item.description: A Florida man was arrested after allegedly calling for the assassination of President Donald Trump on Facebook. The Secret Service is investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tattoo removal expert says 1 in 7 clients asks to remove this specific tattoo
item.description: Today’s tattoo-removal experts are quite adept at ridding their clients of all types of ill-conceived tattoos. But there are a few specific categories of ill-conceived ink that they tend to treat more often than others.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger trial: New revelations in Idaho student murders case as defense grills lead detective in court
item.description: Judge Steven Hippler did not render any decisions, but two days of hearings revealed new information in the Idaho student murders case against Bryan Kohberger.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship would overturn more than a century of precedent
item.description: President Donald Trump has said since his first administration that he wants to end birthright citizenship, a constitutional right for everyone born in the United States. This week he issued an executive order that would eliminate it, upending more than a century of precedent.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: President Trump is reversing his predecessor’s efforts to clean up areas hardest hit by pollution from heavy industry, ports and roadways – communities that are often largely Black, Latino and low-income.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump pumps coal as answer to AI power needs but any boost could be short-lived
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s promise to go all in on fossil fuels includes praise for coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that's long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: ICE agents raid NJ seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Spirit Airlines passengers can now be removed from flight for 'offensive' tattoos, clothing
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines could now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or piece of clothing is deemed offensive, according to the airline's updated policy.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Southern cities get creative to clear leftover snow and ice after rare winter storm
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who laundered money for Chicago-based drug trafficking organization handed 8-year sentence after 15 years on the run
item.description: CHICAGO — A man who laundered money for a Chicago-based drug trafficking organization was handed an eight-year federal prison sentence this week after spending more than a decade on the run.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: 12-year-old boy shot after snowball hit car, police say
item.description: A 12-year-old boy was shot while throwing snowballs Wednesday evening in Hartford, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about President Donald Trump's order targeting the rights of transgender people
item.description: An executive order President Donald Trump signed on his first day back in office offers a new federal government definition of the sexes that could have a major impact on transgender people nationwide.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts worry that Trump's Jan. 6 pardons will legitimize political violence, embolden extremists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After President Donald Trump pardoned around 1,500 Jan. 6 Capitol rioters on Monday, far-right activists cheered the move and said it strengthened their loyalty to him. Some also borrowed from the president’s own rhetoric, calling for retribution.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski to vote against Hegseth; first Republican to oppose a Trump Cabinet pick
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski announced Thursday that she will vote against confirming Pete Hegseth to lead Pentagon, becoming the first Republican to oppose one of President Donald Trump's Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ship freed after being stuck in ice since Wednesday
item.description: As of 3 p.m. Saturday, the boat was traveling at seven knots, which is equivalent to roughly eight miles per hour.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
